common_issues.likely_cause: Downstream service degradation or connection pool saturation
diagnostic_steps: Check error rates on downstream services (auth, user, order)
Check active connection count — should be < 5000
Check thread pool utilization metric
Review recent deployments on dependent services
common_issues.remediation: If downstream issue, fix downstream first. If gateway overload, scale replicas to 6+.

common_issues.likely_cause: Target service is down or misconfigured
diagnostic_steps: Identify which route prefix is failing from logs
Check health of the target service
Verify service discovery entries are correct
common_issues.remediation: Restart target service, or rollback if recent deployment.

common_issues.likely_cause: Redis cache unavailable or session store overloaded
diagnostic_steps: Check Redis connectivity and latency
Check auth-service memory usage — JWT parsing leak known in v2.3.x
Check active session count
common_issues.remediation: If memory leak: rollback to last stable version. If Redis: failover to replica.

common_issues.likely_cause: Known memory leak in JWT token cache (versions 2.3.0-2.3.4)
diagnostic_steps: Check deployed version — vulnerable range is 2.3.0 to 2.3.4
Run memory profiler diagnostic
Check GC pause times
common_issues.remediation: Rollback to v2.2.x or upgrade to v2.3.5+. Restart will provide temporary relief.

common_issues.likely_cause: Database replica lag or cache miss storm
diagnostic_steps: Check replica replication lag (should be < 100ms)
Check Redis hit rate for user cache
Check database-replica CPU and connections
common_issues.remediation: If replica lag: direct reads to primary temporarily. If cache: warm cache from replica.

common_issues.likely_cause: Database connection pool exhausted or payment-service timeout
diagnostic_steps: Check database-primary active connections — pool max is 200
Check order-service connection pool utilization
Check payment-service health and latency
Review recent deployments
common_issues.remediation: If connection leak: rollback deployment, then restart connection pool. If payment timeout: increase timeout or activate circuit breaker.

common_issues.likely_cause: Connection leak — connections acquired but never released
diagnostic_steps: Run connection_pool_status diagnostic on order-service
Check for recent deployments that modified DB layer
Compare connection acquisition vs release rates
common_issues.remediation: Rollback the deployment that introduced the leak. Restart database-primary to clear stale connections.

common_issues.likely_cause: External payment gateway timeout or database write failure
diagnostic_steps: Check external gateway connectivity
Check database-primary write latency
Check for data inconsistencies between payment records and order records
common_issues.remediation: If gateway: enable fallback provider. If DB: check for schema migration issues.

common_issues.symptom: Data inconsistency between payment and order records
common_issues.likely_cause: Race condition during blue-green deployment, or stale cache reads
diagnostic_steps: Run data_consistency_check diagnostic
Check if multiple versions were running simultaneously
Verify cache invalidation is working on deployment
Check transaction isolation levels
common_issues.remediation: Stop dual-version traffic. Run data reconciliation. Fix affected records via targeted update.

common_issues.likely_cause: Consumer throughput reduced or message-queue overloaded
diagnostic_steps: Check message-queue depth and consumer count
Check notification-service processing rate
Check for upstream service flooding queue with retries
common_issues.remediation: Scale notification-service consumers. If upstream flood: fix upstream retry storm first.

common_issues.likely_cause: Connection leak in a client service or traffic spike
diagnostic_steps: Run connection_pool_status diagnostic — check per-service connection counts
Identify which service holds the most connections
Check if connection count is growing without releases
Check for long-running queries holding connections
common_issues.remediation: Fix the leaking service first (usually rollback). Then restart database to clear stale connections.

common_issues.likely_cause: Lock contention, vacuum running, or replication backpressure
diagnostic_steps: Check active lock count and wait events
Check if autovacuum is running
Check WAL generation rate
common_issues.remediation: If locks: identify and kill blocking queries. If vacuum: let it complete or increase resources.

common_issues.likely_cause: Heavy write load on primary or replica resource constraint
diagnostic_steps: Check replica lag metric
Check replica CPU and I/O utilization
Check WAL receiver status
common_issues.remediation: If resource issue: scale replica. If persistent: consider adding another replica.

common_issues.likely_cause: Cache capacity exceeded or TTLs not set properly
diagnostic_steps: Check memory usage vs max memory
Check eviction rate
Check hit/miss ratio
common_issues.remediation: Increase maxmemory or fix TTL policy on hot keys.

common_issues.likely_cause: Consumer failure, or producer flooding due to upstream retry storm
diagnostic_steps: Check consumer count and processing rate
Check producer rate — is it abnormally high?
Check for poison messages in DLQ
common_issues.remediation: If consumer issue: restart consumers. If producer flood: fix upstream retry logic.
